steps.title: Identify the structures around the blank
steps.content: Left side: an independent clause (“the student discovered biographical sketches of two other Black Americans who served in Congress”). Right side: a noun phrase listing two specific people.

steps.title: Apply the rule for introducing lists
steps.content: A colon introduces a list or elaboration—and here we have exactly that: a list of names. A period (A) creates a fragment; a semicolon (C) is for independent clauses, but the right side is a noun phrase; lowercase “congress” (D) violates proper noun rules. This matches choice B — B is correct.
